Empires & Puzzles Betty Review: The Fluffy Ninja Gargoyle

Don't let the cotton candy exterior fool you. Betty brings the Gargoyle's legendary stone skin to the 3-star arena. But is her 90% damage reduction enough to offset her pillow-soft attack? We break down the stats, the 'Softskin' mechanic, and why she might just be the most annoying flank in Rush Tournaments.
Betty Review: Is This Plushie The Ultimate 3-Star Tank?
Betty, The Softskin Shinobi: When Plushies Fight Back

Meet Betty, the 3-star Dark (Purple) hero from the "Return to Sanctuary" Gargoyle event. While her brethren look like terrifying creatures of stone and obsidian, Betty looks like she wandered out of a
casting call after falling into a vat of cotton candy.

Despite her adorable, plush-toy appearance, she is a Rogue class hero who brings the frustratingly effective "Softskin" mechanic—previously reserved for legendary heavy hitters—into the 3-star arena. She plays a support-tank hybrid role, designed less to kill you with damage and more to annoy you into submission by turning your powerful snipes into gentle tickles.

Let's address the elephant (or rather, the furry purple monster) in the room. Betty does not look like a Gargoyle. She looks like a high-level piñata that learned ninjutsu. The color palette is a vibrant mix of purple and blue fluff, complete with a ninja mask and golden kunai. It is a stark, hilarious contrast to the grim, stony aesthetic of the original Gargoyle family. One has to wonder if she was intended for a "Mighty Pets" crossover and took a wrong turn at the portal. She is undeniably the "fluffiest" killer in the Dark element roster.

.
In the modern landscape of 3-star heroes, specifically compared to Toons and the latest Construct or Astral Elf heroes, Betty is... average.
Her 530 attack is underwhelming. For context, modern 3-star hitters often push 550-600 base attack.
Her HP (783) is decent, but her defense (477) is on the softer side. She relies heavily on her Softskin mechanic for survival rather than raw stat density.
She trails behind the stat-bloated "Toon" costumes of Season 1 heroes, but she holds her own against most classic and older event heroes.
"Pointy Darts" is a deceptively named skill. At Average speed, dealing
210% damage to the target and nearby enemies
is frankly weak. You aren't bringing Betty to wipe the board.
The real meat of the skill is the secondary effect:
For the nearby allies, this usually means the
they take damage, it is reduced by 90%, and they resist status ailments. It's a "Get Out of Jail Free" card for one hit.

Unlike Betty herself (who gets multiple charges from her passive), the allies typically only get protection for a single instance of damage. This makes it excellent for blocking a massive snipe from a hero like Kvasir or Bane, but useless against rapid-fire minions or damage-over-time (DoT).
to charge (9 with a level 17 mana troop or adequate bonuses, though mana troops don't exist in 3-star tournaments, so you are looking at standard speed unless in Rush).
With a base attack of 530, a 210% hit deals roughly 1,113 damage
. Against a typical 3-star with ~500 defense, this translates to a scratch—maybe 200-250 damage per hero. It's chip damage, nothing more.
Reducing a 1000 damage snipe to 100 damage is effectively "healing" 900 HP. This is where her mathematical value lies.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
Betty's kit is defined by her passive. Without it, she would be food.

The Gargoyle family bonus provides immunity to Poison, Burn, and Water damage depending on the number of heroes. In the 3-star arena where heroes like Bagreg (Burn) or various poisoners exist, this is a nice niche perk, but rarely a game-winner on its own.
This is the star of the show. Every time Betty fires, she grants
She cannot be Defense Downed, Poisoned, or Mindless Attacked while the skin is active.
This is critical. You cannot dispel it; you have to hit through it.

This is a disappointing choice for her. Betty is a survivalist support hero. Giving her an Attack Up Aether power feels like putting a spoiler on a tractor. It helps her weak snipe slightly, but a defensive power (like Damage Reduction or Regen) would have been far superior.
Released during the "Return to Sanctuary" event, Betty brought the high-end Gargoyle mechanic to the pauper's league (3-star rarity).
While her stats aren't breaking records, her
is timeless. 90% damage reduction scales infinitely. Whether the enemy hits for 500 or 5000, 90% reduction is still 90% reduction.
Betty is not a universal plug-and-play hero. She shines brilliantly in

and specific tank/flank positions where her survivability can stall out an offense. In standard play, her Average speed and low damage make her a liability compared to faster, harder-hitting options like the Toons.
Since Betty's damage is negligible, there is no reason to buff her attack stat. You want her to survive long enough to fire and refresh her Softskin.
Maximizes her survivability. Higher defense makes the 10% damage she
Her special skill will hit like a wet noodle (even more than usual).
She dies faster, meaning fewer Softskin activations. Not recommended.
. As a Rogue, she also has a chance to Dodge. A Dodging, Softskinned tank is a nightmare to kill.
With Softskin active, she is virtually unkillable by direct damage for 4 hits.
protecting neighbors from one big hit can swing a match.
While Softskin is up, she ignores defense down, mana control, and DoT application.
Dodge + Softskin = maximum frustration for the attacker.
She applies no offensive pressure. If she is the last hero standing, you will likely lose by timeout or lack of damage.
Minions (like those from Kvasir) attack multiple times. Each minion hit counts as one "hit" for Softskin, stripping her protection instantly.
Average speed is slow for the 3-star meta, which is dominated by Fast and Very Fast heroes.
This is her playground. At Very Fast speed, she can keep Softskin up almost permanently, making her an incredible Tank or Flank.
Since healing is disabled, damage mitigation (Softskin) is the next best thing. She prevents damage rather than healing it.
A solid backup for clean-up teams where you need to survive a specific sniper.
Betty is best used as a "stall" tactic. She buys time for your slower, heavier hitters to charge up.
Double Treevil is standard, but putting Betty in the flanks protects the Treevils. If the enemy tries to snipe the Treevil, Betty's buff (if fired) might save them.
Betty protects the squishier hitters. Kvasir provides minions to strip the enemy's protection while Betty preserves your team.
(and any minion maker) is her hard counter. 3 minions hitting Betty will strip 3 layers of Softskin in a single turn.
remove Softskin, so don't bring them for that purpose.
Avoid pairing her with heroes who overwrite buffs if there is a conflict, though Softskin is unique. Don't rely on her as your primary damage source.

If you pull Betty, lock her and level her. She is a unique tool. She might not help you farm the map quickly (low damage), but she will win you raid tournaments that you have no business winning by simply refusing to die.

She is a luxury toy for your 3-star tournament roster. If you take 3-star tournaments seriously, she is worth the Limit Break (LB1) to bulk her up. LB2 (Alpha Aether) is questionable due to the "Attack Up" power, but the stat boost helps her tanking capability.
The community reaction to Betty was... confused.
Players immediately noted, "They all remind me of monsters from
Many refused to call them Gargoyles, suggesting "Pinatas" or "Plushies" instead.

Initial impressions were mixed due to her low damage ("210% damage to 3 is below what Chochin, a season 2 character did"), but opinions shifted once players faced her in Rush tournaments: "Betty rocks the 3* PvP on offense as a flank. Softskin is very impactful."

Betty is a weird, wonderful, and surprisingly effective hero. She looks like she should be hugging you, but she plays like a brick wall. While she will never win an award for "Highest Damage Dealer," she takes the gold medal for "Most Annoying Fluffball." In the cutthroat world of 3-star Rush tournaments, surviving four hits with 90% reduction is often more valuable than dealing 300% damage.
A must-have for tournament enthusiasts, a skip for map farmers.
A comprehensive review of Betty, the 3-star Dark Gargoyle hero. We analyze her Softskin mechanics, stats, and viability in Rush Tournaments and 3-star events.
